title: Unit Final: Privacy, Security & Cyber Safety

questions.prompt: What is the primary purpose of online privacy settings?

choices.text: To make accounts look more attractive
choices.feedback: Privacy settings are not for appearance.

choices.text: To control who can see and access personal information
choices.feedback: Correct. Privacy settings help manage visibility and protect personal data.

choices.text: To increase the number of followers
choices.feedback: Privacy settings are not designed for popularity.

choices.text: To automatically delete old posts
choices.feedback: Privacy settings do not typically delete content automatically.

questions.prompt: Sharing personal information like your full name and address online can increase safety risks.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. Sharing sensitive information can expose you to risks.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Sharing personal details can create safety concerns.

questions.prompt: Which password is the most secure?

choices.text: password123
choices.feedback: This is weak and easily guessed.
This is a common and insecure password.

choices.text: MyName2024
choices.feedback: Personal information makes passwords easier to guess.

choices.text: T$9v!Q2#rP
choices.feedback: Correct. Strong passwords use a mix of characters and are hard to guess.

choices.text: Using multiple usernames for one account
choices.feedback: MFA does not involve multiple usernames.

choices.text: A method that requires more than one form of verification to access an account
choices.feedback: Correct. MFA adds an extra layer of security.

choices.text: Sharing passwords with trusted friends
choices.feedback: Passwords should never be shared.

choices.text: Saving passwords in public documents
choices.feedback: This is unsafe and increases risk.

questions.prompt: Phishing attempts often try to trick users into giving personal information.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. Phishing is designed to steal sensitive information.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Phishing specifically targets personal data.

questions.prompt: Which is a common sign of a phishing message?

choices.text: A message from a known contact with clear language
choices.feedback: This is not typically a phishing indicator.

choices.text: A message with urgent language asking for personal information
choices.feedback: Correct. Urgency and requests for personal data are common phishing signs.

choices.text: A verified website with secure login
choices.feedback: Verified sites are generally safer.

choices.text: A message from a school teacher with normal instructions
choices.feedback: This does not indicate phishing.

questions.prompt: A student receives a link to download free software from an unknown source. What is the safest action?

choices.text: Download it quickly before it expires
choices.feedback: This increases risk of malware.

choices.text: Share the link with friends first
choices.feedback: Sharing unsafe links spreads risk.

choices.text: Avoid clicking and verify the source before downloading
choices.feedback: Correct. Always verify before downloading.

choices.text: Download it and delete it if it looks suspicious
choices.feedback: Damage may already occur after downloading.

questions.prompt: Which action best helps prevent cyberbullying?

choices.text: Responding with more negative comments
choices.feedback: This escalates the situation.

choices.text: Ignoring all communication permanently
choices.feedback: Ignoring may not always resolve the issue.

choices.text: Reporting harmful behavior and seeking support
choices.feedback: Correct. Reporting helps stop harmful behavior.

choices.text: Sharing the messages publicly
choices.feedback: This can make the situation worse.

questions.prompt: Downloading files only from trusted sources helps reduce security risks.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. Trusted sources reduce the risk of malware.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Untrusted downloads increase security risks.

questions.prompt: A student notices unusual activity on their account. What should they do first?

choices.text: Ignore it and continue using the account
choices.feedback: Ignoring can allow the issue to worsen.

choices.text: Share the account with a friend for help
choices.feedback: Sharing accounts is unsafe.

choices.text: Immediately change the password and review account security
choices.feedback: Correct. Securing the account quickly is critical.

choices.text: Post about it publicly online
choices.feedback: This does not resolve the issue and may increase risk.
